turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: the case is submitted We'll hear argument next in Number oh oh ten seventy-two, Leonard Edelman versus Lynchburg College. Mr. Schnapper. [Inaudible]

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court. The relation-back rule for verification under section sixteen oh one point twelve B is a proper exercise of the authority granted to the EEOC under section seven thirteen of title seven to promulgate procedural regulations. The regulation is consistent with the language of section seven oh six. Title seven requires that a charge be verified, and the statute also requires that a charge be filed within a hundred and eighty or three hundred days of the act of discrimination. But as Judge Luttig correctly observed below, these two statutory requirements are independent of each other. Specifically, section seven oh six E one establishes deadlines of a hundred and eighty or three hundred days, but it applies those deadlines only to when, quote, a charge must be filed. Section seven oh six E one does not purport to establish a deadline for verification. Conversely, section seven oh six B requires verification, but it contains no deadline for doing so. That omission is particularly significant because other requirements which are contained in section seven oh six B do have deadlines. [Inaudible]

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: What what about the other elements that that that have that that are required by the statute to be in the in the cla- in the charge? Surely the commission has to serve notice of the charge, including the date, place and circumstances of the alleged unlawful employment practice. Isn't that statutory provision implicitly a requirement that the charge contain the date, place, and circumstances of the alleged unlawful practice?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: O- I wouldn't go quite that far. The the the commission's interpretation of that, which is embodied in section sixteen oh one point twelve B, concludes that a charge is sufficient if it identifies the parties and contains a description of the alleged discriminatory practice.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Well, how how can how can the statute be complied with? I mean, the statute, two thousand E dash five B, clearly says that the commission on receiving a charge shall serve a notice of it, paren, including the date, place, and circumstances of the alleged unlawful employment practice...

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Well, it does... on the employer.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: it does so provide.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: So, you you think the...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: charge doesn't even have to e- c- because the commission hasn't chosen to require that, a charge doesn't even have to contain the date, circumstances, and place of the alleged we-

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, the commission has...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: What does it have to contain?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The regulation...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I have...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: been discriminated against?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: It I I understand the regulation to require more specificity than that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: No. I understand that.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: But but what you say about about the requirement or or nonrequirement of of of of of of oath, of of it being under oath, I think you you you're going to have to logically say about other requirements or, a- a- a- as you think them, nonrequirements of the charge.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: I I think that you have to read well, the com- the commission's view is that there is a- a- an irreducible minimum that has to be in a charge.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Why?

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Why does it come to that position? It just made it up?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The statute expressly provides that the commission can specify what information needs to be in a charge, and that's the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I see. Not...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: because there's any statutory compulsion, but just in its in its wisdom and beneficence, the commission has decided that there has to be a certain minimal amount of information in the charge,

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I I I I don't know f- f-

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Federal Register recounts any any explanation, but I- it seems to me it would be logical for the commission to have looked at that the provision to which you refer in in in framing the...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: the requirements it has.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: provision to which I refer, it would say a charge has to I'm, we're not going to bother an employer and require him to come back with a response unless y- you you haven't just come in and said, I've been discriminated against.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Well, w- w- what happens as a practical matter where verification occurs after the filing date is that the employer is usually not required to actually take any action.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: But here what the employer wasn't even no- wasn't notified until the form on the EEOC's proper form that did everything, including the verification.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Your Honor, i- it's our view that the that the court of that the EEOC erred in not providing notice at that time, and it's I think it's clear, from a reading of the compliance manual, that its own manual did require notice at that time.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: And and would also require notice of of of of a charge that was imperfect in other respects within ten days of receiving the charge.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: If you...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: a telephone in the the requirement that it be in writing, for example, that's a requirement the that isn't isn't particularly in the in the statute of limitations provision, but it's a requirement that it be in writing.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: contact the employer about that.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The it's our view that that that if the commission receives a sufficient charge, it must do so.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: This is all...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: just a matter of the regulations.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The the the statute requires that a charge be filed, so I think it would have to be reduced to writing.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: But I, but I was saying...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: filed.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: But I would say the...

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: commission probably could alter its its its regulation and deem a memorialized phone call the filing of the charge.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: here.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: And and to to get back to the specific issue before the Court, the Fifth Circuit excuse me the Fourth Circuit would have reached the same result in this case regardless of whether this letter, notice of this letter or the letter itself had been served on the employer within ten days of the receipt of the letter on November fourteenth.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Mr. Schnapper, do you agree that if you started out with Judge Luttig's position.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: It remains...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: those four questions are alive and would have to be...

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: It it th- it remains open to the to the respondent to raise those issues on remand, and and we think that that's the appropriate procedure...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Thank you.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: f- for addressing it.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Do you do you think it's...

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: good practice for the EEOC to wait until after the three hundred day period and a verification before it even notifies...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: No.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: No, they, it was in in our view, it was improper to have done that here, that that the the the statute doesn't authorize them to await verification.

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: Okay.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: does identify some circumstances in which there might be delay in in in notification.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Let me suppose you have a statutory provision which says that the complaint shall be in writing, sworn to under oath, shall set forth the time, place, and circumstances of the alleged grievance, comma, and shall be presented to the agency within one hundred days after the alleged grievance.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I I...

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: any boy, I would never read a statute that way.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I don't, well...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: that that that that go that far?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: The the Federal rules require that a notice of appeal shall be in writing and shall be filed within a certain period of time.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: I- In these other situations that you mentioned, does the court take action against a particular individual or require a response from a particular individual before the verification occurs? You see, that that that that seems to me the the difference here, that you're you're saying the commission, within ten days after re- receiving this unverified complaint, has to submit it to the employer and ask the employer to respond to it.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: No.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: which requires an answer within so many days. It's simply a heads-up.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Now, d- does the agency require verification to occur before it will demand a response from the employer?

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I think that would...

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: be the normal practice.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Isn't that what the form that EEOC...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: sends out, as you've described the procedure? It's called form five, or whatever it is, that they send to the complainant says sign, verify.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: That's that's that's what occurred here.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: do anything. It's not like a complaint.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: meanwhile the the I mean, this this could occur a very long time after the event occurred...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I think...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: commission waits that long to get the verification.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I think...

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: not in practice.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: time.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Miss Blatt, we'll hear from you.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Chief Justice, and may it please the Court.

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: But but i- but in this case, the a- the agency doesn't treat it as a full charge until it's verified for purposes of notifying the employer.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, that's not fo-

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: you you s- you you you you want us to accept this argument as to what a charge is, but then you don't want us to accept it for when the employer has to know about it so the evidence doesn't go stale.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: It's not that the it's not that the agency is waiting for verification before it gives notice, and if the only thing missing from a charge is verification, the agency's procedures require notice within ten days.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, but...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: aren't, shouldn't the two be be tied together? I mean, it's reasonable to have it relate back so long as there is no prejudice to the to the employer from having it relate back.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: But, Justice Scalia, the the agency's procedures required this employer to have notice, whether or not the November fourteenth letter was verified, and the employer then can preserve its documents or or respond to the charge or do or do whatever it wants.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: The...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: that's a separate mistake, and whatever prejudice comes from that the employer is free to raise on remand.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: But you agree with Mr. Schnapper that the agency just missed, that they should have given f- to for the purpose of giving giving notice, sent that unverified charge...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: to the employer.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: absolutely.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: What about the argument that B comes before E, so and to define what a charge is, the word charge, and then i- it's given a certain description in B.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: Well, title seven didn't define the word charge as a verified charge.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: And in the normal...

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: course of business, the agency will try to obtain a standard form whose signature line contains an affirmation, and so the verification requirement will be supplied.

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: C- Can you tell me what happens, j- it's not this case if there's a rule in the in the circuits, I don't think we passed on it.

turns.name: LISA S. BLATT
turns.text: Yeah, we know of just a handful of cases where the charge just got lost in the system, and w- the consequences of that would turn on two factors.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Miss Blatt.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: [Inaudible]

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: In the Mohasco case, this Court taught that the EEOC may not adopt regulations that are inconsistent with the statutory mandate.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Judge...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Judge Luttig said that that was a very plausible argument.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: O- Justice Ginsburg, we don't believe that that is a permissible reading of the statute.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: There's simply no...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: that the reading that you are saying is the only proper reading is s- somewhat inconsistent with this Court's position in Becker against Montgomery last t- term.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Justice Ginsburg, we we don't think so because we think what happened in Becker was a a a harmonization of two rules of the Court, with a focus particularly on rule eleven, which in the same rule which established the requirement of the of the signing, established the the method to cure a failure to sign.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I think we we heard...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: the argument from Mr. Schnapper and Miss Blatt that as a background common law principle, the the idea of a curative amendment to provide a signature, to provide verification that then relates back is nothing new.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Your Honor, I I believe that the background principle, if you will, the background legal principle of our federalism and the background legal principle of due process to employers in fairness to employers in giving notice, was probably more a part of the applicable legal background here...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: But that was conceded, the...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: That the the the commission should have sent the imperfect charge.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Well, Your Your Honor, the the regulation here eliminates the filing deadline.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: But that's not what anybody is urging here.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Well, Your Honor, there wa- the the letter itself was never verified.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: There was a form five that was verified.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: like an amended complaint.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: There's no indication that Congress here adopted common law pleading rules.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Do you question,

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Bell, your opponents' descriptions of the common law pleading background that a complaint that was f- s- s- was required by rule to be verified filed not verified could be verified later?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: There are certainly many cases that hold that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: put out.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEVENS
turns.text: may I ask, that , going back to Justice Scalia's questions e- earlier? The statute requires that the charges shall be in writing, under oath or ath- affirmation, and shall contain such information and and be in such form as the commission requires.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE STEVENS
turns.text: So that if they if they add a requirement, more information after there's a filing, they would also be deciding that the original charge was un- was untimely.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Well, and in this case, Your Honor, that's exactly what they did.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: charge. They kept...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: writing letters to that effect.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: do something.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: also have to say, Mr. Bell, that that since the complaint or the charge has to be under the statute in such form as the commission shall prescribe, you'd also have to say that if the if the charge was filed on nine by twelve paper and the and the commission had prescribed eight and a half by twelve, that it's ineffective.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Yes, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Any any little any little foot fault...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: would...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Any little...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: foot fault would would render it ineffective.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: I think that that...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: that...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: little technical detail that that wasn't exactly as the commission's...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: rules required.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: by the commission, I think that's what the statute says.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Mr. Bell, wouldn't that be, wouldn't that be...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: totally inconsistent with what Congress envisioned? That is, these complaints with the EEOC were not going to be filed by lawyers, lawyers who have leeway to amend under the Federal rules.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: That would be very odd.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: the statute seems to indicate that with respect to the oh, at least with respect to the oath and writing requirements.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: next year perhaps eliminating the writing requirement.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: when not whether.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: But you...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: do have to do it withi- in the method and in the time set out in rule eleven.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: What was filed in the court of appeals within the time that you had to file the notice lacked a signature.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Yes...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: thing that's here within the thirty, three hundred days.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Well, w- we see a fundamental difference between this Court harmonizing rules over which this Court has control, and the Court deciding whether to apply requirements set out in a statute that Congress used to create a certain right.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: You're you're not asserting that our reading of our own rules is unreasonable, are you?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: No, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Well, but if our reading of our rules isn't unreasonable and this agency has simply read the statute the way we read our rules, then I assume that this agency's reading of the statute is not unreasonable.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Your Honor, I, there are two reason we don't think Chevron is the correct, Chevron would uphold this regulation.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: that's embodied in the statute.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: the Government has asserted that that's not the case, and I'm a I, if that if, I I I think we we we have to accept that unless you have solid evidence to the contrary that that in fact they think the notice is normally given and should have been given when here when the when the charge was received...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: They have said that today.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: I I and I...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: of course saw their...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Well, unless you know...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: for sure that it's not.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: And I agree with you that if if if that is not the policy and if if they're being inconsistent in viewing it as a charge for one purpose but not as a charge for another purpose, then then we have a different problem.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Well, a- again, I I refer the Court to the Government's brief in support of amicus , o- of of of, sorry of granting the writ, pages o- sixteen and seventeen.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Mr. Bell, a lawyer...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: representing the Government made a representation before the Court this morning, that the EEOC should have immediately sent that charge to the employer.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: That does, I mean, we-, I I'm not arguing with that.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, now it's clarified...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: what its position is.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: I I understand.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Mr. Bell, this was a deferral case.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Yes, Justice Ginsburg.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: in this case anything had been done at the State agency level?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Your Honor, th- the charge was not sent to the State until it was put under oath...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, that same...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: the f- the same formal whatever they called it, form X, wh- when it was sent to the employer was also sent to the State agency.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: That's correct.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I- Is it...

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: Is the a- a- a- a- apparent inconsistency between the, what the Government says at page sixteen of the b- b- b- brief in su- in support of granting the petition and its representation here ex- explained by the fact that at p- page sixteen the Government says the complaint must be, the charge must be verified before it requires a response,

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I, I see that...

turns.name: JUSTICE KENNEDY
turns.text: another requirement for simply notifying the employer that the charge has been made? It seems to me that's,

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: it...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: the only requirement in the handbook that we saw, I mean, the the the handbook that was filed, that we received on Friday does not mention oath at all.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: that in...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: I don't understand what you said.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: If e- e- e-

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: charge if the charge is received within fifteen days of the...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: As I read this manual...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Yeah...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: whether it was just served o- served on us on Friday, that's that's exactly what it says.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Where where they will give notice of an unperfected charge.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: They won't give notice unless the charge is received...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Right.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: the end of a deadline.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: That that is the way I read of, th- this handbook.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: interesting...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: in looking at the handbook, Your Honor,

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Do you have a section of the handbook where that's...

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: I think it's, i- yes, sir.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: That that's not a page.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: page. The...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: page is down the bottom.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: two dash one...

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: two dash one? Let me try that.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: is what what I have.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: You, what you have just said, suggests that this compliance manual is i- is in need of amendment, but the compliance manual, unlike the regulation that we have, is not something that gets Chevron deference.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: I understand,

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: and this of course, this regulation was not passed with notice and comment rulemaking either, which under the Mead decision gives it less weight than it might have if i- if the regulation...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: I thought the Congress authorized the EEOC to make procedural rules, didn't it?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: They did.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: And that's what these are.

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: By other proof e- Your Honor, I suppose w- w- we lawyers could differ on whether it's a procedural or substantive rule.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well, what would you call rule fifteen C of the Federal Rules of Civil...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Procedure?

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: fifteen...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: C of the Federal Rules...

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Yes.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: Is that a rule of procedure? The relation-back rule?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: , I, Yes, Your Honor, it is.

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: Well, I'm I'm not sure that Mead does does, I don't un-, does does Mead say that that even rules adopted without notice and comment are entitled to Chevron deference so long as they were authorized? I mean, I thought all rules had to be authorized whether whether they do notice and comment or not.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Well,

turns.name: JUSTICE SCALIA
turns.text: starting point, isn't it?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: and one of the problems with this rule is that there's no delegation of authority to the agency to define the term charge, which is, in essence, what they've done.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: But how would you distinguish...

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: from the point of view of the claimant who is filing the original charge, sends a letter, as here, and then the EEOC, if it were super-efficient, would have gotten out the the form in good time?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: It did in this case, Your Honor.

turns.name: JUSTICE GINSBURG
turns.text: How many days did he have left?

turns.name: ALEXANDER W. BELL
turns.text: He had it for approximately a month, at least three weeks.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Bell.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Mr. Schnapper, you have five minutes remaining.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: Thank you, Your Honor.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: non ver-

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: manual say about verification, later verification?

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: I don't believe the manual addresses it.

turns.name: UNKNOWN
turns.text: when service is to...

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: one might infer that then that l- post-filing verification i- is not authorized.

turns.name: ERIC SCHNAPPER
turns.text: It's expressly authorized by the regulation in this case.

turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE REHNQUIST
turns.text: Thank you, Mr. Schnapper.

turns.name: THE MARSHAL
turns.text: The honorable court is now adjourned until tomorrow at ten o'clock.
